title: (No AI) Google’s latest Pixel drop allows Gemini to order groceries for you and more
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Google Pixel 10 Pro showing back panel on a grid of green rectangles](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/257917_Google_Pixel_10_review_AJohnson_0007.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google is [adding several new features](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/devices/pixel/march-2026-pixel-drop/) to Pixel phones with its latest March update, including the ability for its Gemini AI assistant to do things for you, like order groceries or book a ride. This feature, which was [first shown off during Samsung's Unpacked](https://www.theverge.com/tech/884210/google-gemini-samsung-s26-pixel-10-uber) event last week, is rolling out now to the Pixel 10, Pixel 10 Pro, and Pixel 10 Pro XL.

With Gemini's new agentic feature, you can ask the assistant to complete work on your behalf inside "select" apps, including Uber and Grubhub. It will work in the background while you use your phone, but Google notes that you can supervise or interrupt its work at any time. This feature will also be …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/888295/google-gemini-pixel-drop-march-2026)

Google is adding several new features to Pixel phones with its latest March update, including the ability for its Gemini AI assistant to do things for you, like order groceries or book a ride. This feature, which was first shown off during Samsung's Unpacked event last week, is rolling out now to the Pixel 10, Pixel 10 Pro, and Pixel 10 Pro XL.
With Gemini's new agentic feature, you can ask the assistant to complete work on your behalf inside "select" apps, including Uber and Grubhub. It will work in the background while you use your phone, but Google notes that you can supervise or interrupt its work at any time. This feature will also be  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

This feature, which was first shown off during Samsung's Unpacked event last week, is rolling out now to the Pixel 10, […]

title: (No AI) How the experts figure out what’s real in the age of deepfakes
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Mushroom cloud with digital ASCII effect.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/06/STKS515_CYBER_WARFARE_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

You can help fight the spread of fake news by following these newsroom tips. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images

In the days that followed the US and Israel's joint [military strike on Iran on Saturday](https://www.theverge.com/policy/886940/us-israel-attack-iran), floods of images and videos that supposedly document the war have appeared online. Some are old or depict unrelated conflicts, are made or [manipulated with AI](https://verafiles.org/articles/fact-check-video-of-burj-khalifa-collapsing-iran-missile-attack-ai-generated), and in some cases, are actually taken from [military-themed video games like _War Thunder_](https://www.houstonchronicle.com/news/politics/article/greg-abbott-iran-video-post-21950767.php).

With misinformation spreading like wildfire, many people have placed their trust in reputable digital investigators. Organizations like _The New York Times_, [_Indicator_](https://indicator.media/), and [_Bellingcat_](https://www.bellingcat.com/) have extensive verification procedures to avoid publishing synthetic or misleading content. "Audiences can turn to trusted, in …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/888303/photo-video-fake-news-verification-nyt-bellingway)

In the days that followed the US and Israel's joint military strike on Iran on Saturday, floods of images and videos that supposedly document the war have appeared online. Some are old or depict unrelated conflicts, are made or manipulated with AI, and in some cases, are actually taken from military-themed video games like War Thunder. 
With misinformation spreading like wildfire, many people have placed their trust in reputable digital investigators. creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) Summon This AI Agent by Speaking Its Wake Word Mid-Phone Call
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth
content: Deutsche Telekom, the German cell provider—which holds a majority stake in T-Mobile—is partnering with ElevenLabs to enable an AI assistant on all of its network’s calls in Germany. No app required.
contentSnippet: Deutsche Telekom, the German cell provider—which holds a majority stake in T-Mobile—is partnering with ElevenLabs to enable an AI assistant on all of its network’s calls in Germany. No app required.
